title: Comparing Three Generative Families
concept: GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion models all generate new data, but through fundamentally different mechanisms with different strengths and weaknesses.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI tools can create a brand-new picture of a cat that never existed before?

options.text: An image generator like a diffusion or GAN model

exercises.explain: Image generators like GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion models are all built to create brand-new content, like pictures of cats that never existed!

cards.title: Three Ways to Create!
cards.body: Imagine three different artists asked to paint a fantasy dragon. One sketches fast and gets critiqued until it's perfect. One compresses the idea into a tiny code then rebuilds it. One starts with random scribbles and slowly cleans them up into art. That's GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion models — three totally different ways AI learns to create!

cards.title: Three Generative Families
cards.body: GAN (Generative Adversarial Network): two AIs compete — one creates fakes, one spots fakes — until the fakes look real. VAE (Variational Autoencoder): squeezes data into a compact code, then rebuilds it, learning the 'essence' of things. Diffusion: starts with pure noise and gradually removes it step-by-step until a clear image appears. All three generate new data, but their inner mechanisms are completely different.

cards.title: Three Kinds of Artists
cards.body: GAN is like a forger and a detective racing each other — the forger gets better by fooling the detective. VAE is like an artist who memorizes the 'summary' of a scene, then repaints it from that summary. Diffusion is like a sculptor who starts with a rough block of marble (noise) and slowly chips away until a statue appears.

cards.title: Same Goal, Different Roads
cards.body: Say you want an AI to generate a new face. A GAN would have a 'Generator' AI make faces while a 'Discriminator' AI judges if they're real or fake, improving both over time. A VAE would compress thousands of real faces into a tiny code, then decode new combinations into fresh faces. A Diffusion model would start with random static and denoise it step-by-step, like Stable Diffusion or DALL-E 2, until a face emerges.

cards.title: Myth: They're All the Same Trick
cards.body: Myth: GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion models are just different names for the same technique. Truth: They use completely different training methods and math! GANs use a competition between two networks, VAEs use compression and probability, and Diffusion uses gradual noise removal. Today's top image generators (like DALL-E and Midjourney) mostly use Diffusion because it tends to make more stable, detailed results — but GANs are often faster, and VAEs are great for learning compact representations.

cards.title: GAN vs Diffusion: Two Popular Approaches
cards.body: Both generate images, but they build them in opposite ways — one is a race, the other is a cleanup job.

left.text: Two AIs compete: a Generator tries to fool a Discriminator, getting better through rivalry. Fast to generate, but training can be unstable.

right.text: Starts with random noise and slowly removes it step-by-step to reveal a clear image. Slower to generate, but very stable and detailed.

exercises.prompt: Which generative model uses two competing AI networks — one that creates fakes and one that tries to spot them?
exercises.explain: GANs work by having a Generator and a Discriminator compete — the Generator tries to make fakes realistic enough to fool the Discriminator.

exercises.prompt: A Diffusion model creates images by starting with random noise and gradually removing it until a clear image appears.
exercises.explain: True! Diffusion models begin with pure noise and denoise it step by step until a clean, detailed image emerges.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: A ___ compresses data into a small code and then rebuilds it to learn the essence of the data.
exercises.explain: A VAE (Variational Autoencoder) compresses data into a compact code (encoding) and then reconstructs it (decoding), learning the key patterns along the way.

exercises.prompt: An image generator makes a picture in one quick step by having two competing networks battle it out during training, but generation itself is fast once trained. Which model is this most likely describing?
exercises.explain: GANs are known for fast generation once trained, since the Generator just produces an image in a single forward pass, unlike Diffusion which takes many steps.

exercises.prompt: You're building an AI art tool and want the most stable training process with highly detailed, realistic outputs, even if generation takes a bit longer. Which generative family should you choose, and why?

options.text: Diffusion model — because it gradually denoises step-by-step, giving stable training and detailed results, even though it's slower to generate
GAN — because it always trains perfectly stably with no issues
VAE — because it never produces blurry results
Any of them work exactly the same way with identical tradeoffs

exercises.explain: Diffusion models are popular for high-quality image generation (like DALL-E and Stable Diffusion) because their step-by-step denoising process tends to be more stable during training and produces very detailed results, though generating each image takes more steps than a GAN.
